name: Amazon EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud)
description: Secure and resizable compute capacity (virtual servers) in the cloud.
examTips: Spot instances for cheap/fault-tolerant workloads.
Reserved Instances for steady-state workloads (1-3 year commitment).
On-Demand for short-term, unpredictable workloads.
Dedicated Hosts for compliance and server-bound software licenses.

name: AWS Lambda
description: Serverless compute service that runs code in response to events and automatically manages the compute resources.
examTips: Maximum execution time is 15 minutes.
Priced by invocation and duration (per millisecond).
Great for event-driven architectures (e.g., S3 triggers, API Gateway).

name: Amazon ECS (Elastic Container Service)
description: Highly scalable, high-performance container orchestration service that supports Docker containers.
examTips: Can run on EC2 instances or AWS Fargate (serverless).
Use Fargate to avoid managing the underlying servers.

name: Amazon S3 (Simple Storage Service)
description: Object storage service offering industry-leading scalability, data availability, security, and performance.
examTips: Standard is for frequently accessed data.
S3 Intelligent-Tiering moves data automatically based on access patterns.
S3 Glacier is for long-term archiving (retrieval times from minutes to hours).
Use multipart upload for files > 100MB (required > 5GB).

name: Amazon EBS (Elastic Block Store)
description: Easy to use, high performance block storage service designed for use with Amazon EC2.
examTips: EBS volumes are AZ-specific. They must be in the same AZ as the EC2 instance.
Snapshots are incremental backups stored in S3.
Provisioned IOPS (io1/io2) for high-performance databases.

name: Amazon EFS (Elastic File System)
description: Simple, serverless, set-and-forget, elastic file system for Linux EC2 instances.
examTips: Can be mounted to multiple EC2 instances across multiple AZs concurrently.
Uses the NFS protocol.
Not supported for Windows instances (use FSx for Windows instead).

name: Amazon FSx
description: Fully managed third-party file systems.
examTips: FSx for Windows File Server (supports SMB, Active Directory).
FSx for Lustre (high-performance file system for HPC and Machine Learning).

name: Amazon RDS (Relational Database Service)
description: Managed relational database service for MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server, and MariaDB.
examTips: Multi-AZ for Disaster Recovery (synchronous replication).
Read Replicas for performance scaling (asynchronous replication).
Automated backups and point-in-time recovery.

name: Amazon Aurora
description: MySQL and PostgreSQL-compatible relational database built for the cloud.
examTips: Up to 5x faster than standard MySQL, 3x faster than standard PostgreSQL.
Storage automatically scales up to 128 TB.
Aurora Global Database for cross-region disaster recovery and low-latency global reads.

name: Amazon DynamoDB
description: Key-value and document database that delivers single-digit millisecond performance at any scale.
examTips: Fully managed NoSQL database, Serverless.
DAX (DynamoDB Accelerator) for microsecond read latency.
Global Tables for active-active cross-region replication.

name: Amazon VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)
description: Logically isolated section of the AWS Cloud where you can launch AWS resources in a virtual network.
examTips: Security Groups act at the instance level (Stateful).
Network ACLs act at the subnet level (Stateless).
VPC Peering connects two VPCs; it is not transitive.

name: Amazon Route 53
description: A highly available and scalable cloud Domain Name System (DNS) web service.
examTips: Routing policies: Simple, Weighted, Latency, Failover, Geolocation, Geoproximity, Multivalue.
Can be used to perform health checks and route traffic to healthy endpoints.

name: Amazon CloudFront
description: Fast content delivery network (CDN) service that securely delivers data, videos, applications, and APIs.
examTips: Caches content at Edge Locations to reduce latency.
Integrates with AWS WAF for security against DDoS and web exploits.
OAC (Origin Access Control) / OAI is used to restrict S3 bucket access to CloudFront only.

name: AWS Global Accelerator
description: Networking service that improves the availability and performance of applications with global users.
examTips: Provides two static Anycast IP addresses.
Routes traffic over the AWS global network (bypassing the public internet) to the closest healthy endpoint.

name: AWS IAM (Identity and Access Management)
description: Manage access to AWS services and resources securely.
examTips: Users, Groups, Roles, and Policies.
Roles are used for delegating permissions (e.g., EC2 instances accessing S3) without using access keys.
Follow the principle of least privilege.

name: AWS KMS (Key Management Service)
description: Create and manage cryptographic keys and control their use across a wide range of AWS services.
examTips: Used for encrypting data at rest (S3, EBS, RDS, etc.).
Customer Managed Keys (CMK) vs AWS Managed Keys.

name: AWS Secrets Manager
description: Helps you protect secrets needed to access your applications, services, and IT resources.
examTips: Built-in secret rotation (using Lambda).
Ideal for database credentials and API keys (compare with Systems Manager Parameter Store which doesn't have native rotation).

name: AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall)
description: Helps protect your web applications or APIs against common web exploits.
examTips: Can be attached to CloudFront, ALB, API Gateway, or AppSync.
Protects against SQL injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), and IP blocks.

name: Amazon SQS (Simple Queue Service)
description: Fully managed message queuing for microservices, distributed systems, and serverless applications.
examTips: Used to decouple applications.
Standard queue (best-effort ordering, at-least-once delivery).
FIFO queue (strict ordering, exactly-once processing).
Visibility timeout prevents other workers from seeing a message being processed.

name: Amazon SNS (Simple Notification Service)
description: Fully managed pub/sub messaging, SMS, email, and mobile push notifications.
examTips: Pub/Sub model (Push-based).
Can fan-out messages to multiple SQS queues or Lambda functions.

name: Amazon EventBridge
description: Serverless event bus that connects application data from your own apps, SaaS, and AWS services.
examTips: The modern replacement for CloudWatch Events.
Used to schedule cron jobs (EventBridge Scheduler) or react to state changes in AWS resources.

name: AWS CloudTrail
description: Tracks user activity and API usage across your AWS infrastructure.
examTips: Answers 'Who did what, when, and from where?'.
Essential for auditing and compliance.

name: Amazon CloudWatch
description: Monitoring and observability service built for DevOps engineers, developers, and IT managers.
examTips: CloudWatch Metrics: Monitor resources (CPU, Network).
CloudWatch Logs: Centralized log storage.
CloudWatch Alarms: Trigger notifications or Auto Scaling actions based on metrics.
